name: Oklo Inc.

profile.description: Oklo is a pre-revenue advanced nuclear company developing compact fast fission power plants (Aurora Powerhouses) for commercial power generation, with a focus on supplying clean, reliable baseload power to AI data centers and defense installations. The company went public in 2024 via SPAC merger with AltC Acquisition Corp (backed by Sam Altman), holds $2.5B in cash following a January 2026 capital raise, and has an ~15 GW order book including agreements with Meta and Switch.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Oklo is developing the Aurora Powerhouse — a compact fast fission reactor designed for modular deployment, using recycled nuclear waste as fuel — representing genuinely breakthrough nuclear technology rather than incremental improvement [2]. The company secured three Aurora nuclear heat production projects at Idaho National Laboratory under the White House's Nuclear Reactor Pilot Program — more than any other company [2]. A $6B+ order book including Meta, Switch, Diamondback Energy, and Wyoming Hyperscale demonstrates commercial validation of the concept [1][2]. No existing commercial company converts nuclear waste to energy at this scale.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: CEO and cofounder Jacob DeWitte has led Oklo since its 2013 founding — a 12-year commitment to commercializing nuclear energy for abundant, clean power [2]. DeWitte's mission of providing 'clean energy at scale, particularly to enable the deployment of AI' aligns directly with the world's most critical infrastructure challenge [2]. Inspire Faith Alignment score of 48 with 20 positive attributions including Energy Management, GHG Emissions, Business Ethics, and Critical Risk Management reflects extraordinary alignment with responsible operations [3].

infrastructure.label: WEAK
infrastructure.analysis: Oklo is pre-revenue with a $139.3M operating loss in 2025 and EPS of -$0.27 in Q4 2025 (missing estimates of -$0.17 by 59%) [1]. The company holds $2.5B in cash post-January 2026 capital raise and guides $80-100M cash burn in 2026. Aurora INL target adjusted to 2028. The liquidity floor test passes (stock ~$55 × avg daily volume), but infrastructure metrics are all deeply negative. Pre-revenue nuclear companies require a distinct scoring framework — the strong balance sheet is the only positive metric.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: Oklo is uniquely positioned as the company directly supplying the energy required to run AI infrastructure — not threatened by AI but fundamentally enabling it [2]. Meta signed a multi-year agreement specifically to provide nuclear power for AI data centers, and analyst Dan Ives predicted 'every Big Tech company' will make a nuclear play in 2026 [2]. AI is the primary demand driver for Oklo's product (baseload power), creating a structural demand tailwind that no competitor or technology can displace.

infinite_game.summary: Oklo embodies infinite game thinking: a decade of patient pre-commercial development, a mission aligned with long-term civilization-scale energy needs, and the structural courage to take on nuclear regulatory complexity when the easier path was to abandon the project. DeWitte's transition to board chair after Sam Altman stepped down to avoid conflict of interest shows institutional integrity.

just_cause.analysis: Oklo's mission — 'providing clean energy at scale to enable the deployment of AI and power the modern economy' — directly addresses what Sam Altman described as the binding constraint on human progress: affordable, accessible, safe, clean energy [2].

trusting_teams.analysis: COO Caroline Cochran and CEO DeWitte cofounder the company in 2013 — 12 years of partnership. The graceful transition of Sam Altman from board chair to strategic ally demonstrates mature governance [2].

worthy_rivals.analysis: Oklo benchmarks against TerraPower (Bill Gates-backed), NuScale, X-Energy, and Kairos Power in the advanced nuclear space, and has explicitly won three of eleven White House Pilot Program projects — more than any competitor [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: The pivot from DoD/defense applications to commercial AI data center power supply represents strategic flexibility. The nuclear waste recycling fuel cycle (closing the nuclear fuel loop) also represents genuine technology innovation vs. conventional nuclear [2].

courage_to_lead.analysis: Building a commercial nuclear company from scratch, navigating NRC licensing, and landing $6B+ order book agreements with Meta and Switch before a single reactor is operational requires extraordinary founder courage [1][2].

investment_thesis: Oklo holds $2.5B in cash, no debt, and ~15 GW of signed power agreements with Meta, Switch, Diamondback Energy, and Wyoming Hyperscale — validating commercial demand before any reactor is built [1][2]. The AI data center power demand crisis is driving hyperscalers to secure long-term nuclear power agreements, and Oklo is the first-mover with commercial-stage agreements. Analyst consensus price target of $97.27 implies 77% upside from $54.96 [1]. The company has three INL pilot program slots — more than any competitor — and construction in Pike County, Ohio is targeting 2026 start.
thesis_continued: Oklo is pre-revenue, pre-license, and burning $80-100M annually [1]. The Aurora INL commercial target has already slipped from 2026 to 2028, introducing execution risk that could push the entire commercial timeline [1]. The stock trades at an extreme premium to any traditional valuation metric — entirely on the future value of an unproven reactor design that still requires NRC licensing. The Q4 2025 EPS miss of -$0.27 vs. -$0.17 expected (59% miss) suggests cost discipline is weaker than expected [1]. Nuclear regulatory timelines have historically run years beyond initial estimates.
key_catalysts: NRC construction and operating license approval for the Aurora Powerhouse — the single most critical milestone; currently targeted for 2026-2027 [1]
Additional hyperscaler agreements following Meta deal — Apple, Microsoft, Google, and Amazon are all potential customers given stated nuclear energy interest [2]
Construction start at Pike County, Ohio facility in 2026 — targeted for the INL pilot program, with the facility scaling to 1.2 GW by 2034 [2]
key_risks: NRC licensing delay — nuclear regulatory timelines historically run 2-5 years beyond initial projections; Aurora INL target already slipped from 2026 to 2028 [1]
Cost overruns — $80-100M annual cash burn is expected to increase as the company expands headcount; $2.5B cash provides ~25 years at current burn, but construction will accelerate spending

Technology risk — compact fast fission reactors using recycled nuclear waste have not been commercially deployed at scale; first-of-a-kind nuclear projects historically experience significant cost and schedule overruns
sources: Oklo Q4 2025 Financial Results and Business Update, Oklo Investor Relations / Simply Wall St, March 2026
Oklo Strategic Agreements, Meta Partnership, and Nuclear Pilot Program, Fortune / CNBC / PR Newswire, 2025-2026
Oklo Faith Alignment and ESG Assessment, Inspire Insight Data, 2026
